claims.claim: NVIDIA's AC-powered DGX B300 reference architecture ships 72 DGX B300 systems in standard racks, each with three 2U rack PDUs
claims.sourceTitle: DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD with DGX B300 Systems, NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand switching and AC Power Reference Architecture
claims.excerpt: Figure 2 shows 72 x NVIDIA DGX B300 PS systems in standard racks each with three (3) 2U rack PDUs for maximum redundancy.

claims.claim: NVIDIA explicitly frames the AC/PDU architecture as the traditional option, distinct from a DC busbar alternative
claims.sourceTitle: DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD with DGX B300 Systems, NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand switching and AC Power Reference Architecture
claims.excerpt: This Reference Architecture is focused on traditional PDU and AC powered EIA racks. DGX SuperPOD with DGX B300 systems is also available for more dense DB Busbar solutions as well.

claims.claim: NVIDIA's DC busbar reference architecture is marketed as the higher-density, higher-efficiency alternative that bypasses traditional per-rack AC PDU design
claims.sourceTitle: DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture
claims.excerpt: With DGX SuperPOD B300, we introduce the MGX-based, DC busbar powered design for the best datacenter density and efficiency.

claims.claim: NVIDIA states legacy datacenters without DC busbar capability can still build with traditional PDU and AC powered racks, implying PDU racks are the fallback/legacy option not the flagship
claims.sourceTitle: DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture
claims.excerpt: For legacy datacenter without the possibility for DC busbar, it is still possible to build SuperPOD with traditional PDU and AC powered EIA racks.

claims.claim: Even NVIDIA's DC busbar bill-of-materials includes 160 rack PDUs across 80 MGX racks (72 with busbar, 8 without), so busbar deployments are not fully PDU-less
claims.sourceTitle: Major Components — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture

claims.claim: NVIDIA's DC busbar architecture bill of materials includes 72 MGX racks with busbar and 8 MGX racks without busbar
claims.sourceTitle: Major Components — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture
claims.excerpt:
72
19” MGX rack with bubar
754-24972-0046-001

claims.claim: NVIDIA's DC busbar reference design puts rack-level power consumption above 50kW per rack
claims.sourceTitle: DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture
claims.excerpt: The rack-level power consumption per rack exceeds 50 kW.

claims.claim: NVIDIA's AC/PDU reference design puts rack-level power consumption at approximately 56kW per rack
claims.sourceTitle: DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD with DGX B300 Systems, NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand switching and AC Power Reference Architecture
claims.excerpt: In our reference design, four DGX B300 fit within a single rack. The rack-level power consumption per rack is ~56kW.

claims.claim: Grand View Research sizes the global data center rack PDU market at $2.81B in 2025, growing to $5.87B by 2033 at 9.7% CAGR, driven by AI/GPU workloads
claims.sourceTitle: Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit Market Report 2033
claims.excerpt: The global data center rack power distribution unit market size was estimated at USD 2.81 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 5.87 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 9.7% from 2026 to 2033. The growth of high-performance computing (HPC), artificial intelligence (AI), and GPU-intensive workloads is driving demand for data center rack power distribution unit market.

claims.claim: Grand View reports non-intelligent PDUs held the largest revenue share of the rack PDU market in 2025
claims.sourceTitle: Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit Market Report 2033
claims.excerpt: By rack type, non-intelligent segment held the largest revenue share in 2025.

claims.claim: North America held 38.0% revenue share of the global rack PDU market
claims.sourceTitle: Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit Market Report 2033
claims.excerpt: North America held 38.0% revenue share of the global data center rack power distribution unit market.

claims.claim: Mordor Intelligence sizes the rack PDU market at $2.78B in 2025 rising to $3.01B in 2026 and $4.62B by 2031 at 8.96% CAGR, citing hyperscale expansion, high-density AI workloads, and liquid cooling adoption
claims.sourceTitle: Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit (PDU) Market Size, Share, Trends
claims.excerpt: The data center rack power distribution unit (PDU) market size is expected to increase from USD 2.78 billion in 2025 to USD 3.01 billion in 2026 and reach USD 4.62 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 8.96% over 2026-2031. Momentum stems from hyperscale expansion, high-density AI workloads, and liquid-cooling adoption

claims.sourceTitle: Data Center PDU Market Size, Share, Latest Trends & Growth Analysis, 2025-2032
claims.excerpt: According to Marketsandmarkets, the data center PDU market size was valued USD 3.94 biliion in 2025 and is project to reach USD 14.23 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 19.5% from 2026 to 2032. The data center PDU market is driven by the expansion of hyperscale and AI-driven data centers, the increasing rack power density

claims.sourceTitle: Data Center PDU Market Size, Share, Latest Trends & Growth Analysis, 2025-2032
claims.excerpt: Data Center PDU Market Size, Share & Trends by Product Level (Rack PDU, Floor PDU), Phase Type (Single-Phase, Three-Phase), Workload Type (Al Workload, General-Purpose IT Workload), End User (Hyperscale, Colocation, Enterprise) - Global Forecast to 2032

claims.claim: Omdia's Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis 2025 uses 2024 as a base year with historical data back to 2017 and forecasts to 2030, but full figures are paywalled

claims.excerpt: Analyzes the Rack PDU Annual Tracker – 2025 update and includes forecast and vendor shares. Uses 2024 as a base year, with historical data back to 2017 and forecasts to 2030.

claims.claim: NVIDIA's DGX GB300 SuperPOD reference architecture is built on scalable units, with each scalable unit containing 8 DGX GB300 systems
claims.unit: DGX GB300 systems per scalable unit
claims.sourceTitle: NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories
claims.excerpt: DGX SuperPOD is built on the concept of scalable units (SU); each SU contains 8 DGX GB300 systems, which enables rapid deployment of DGX SuperPOD of any size.

tensions.text: The curated node's refinement cycle claims the 'GPU pods skip PDUs' doubt was refuted and the value 'confirmed' by vendor reference architectures, but NVIDIA actually publishes parallel architectures: an AC/PDU version (legacy/fallback) and a DC busbar version explicitly marketed as the denser, more efficient flagship design — meaning the highest-density GPU deployments are steered toward bypassing traditional per-rack AC PDUs rather than confirming universal PDU attach.

Even NVIDIA's own 'DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture' bill of materials lists 160 rack PDUs across 80 MGX racks, so the refutation is only partial: busbar racks still consume some rPDUs, but at a materially lower ratio than the AC architecture's 3 PDUs per rack for 72 racks — a nuance the node's binary 'confirmed' framing elides.

Three independent PDU market-size estimates for the same base year (2025) diverge meaningfully: Grand View ($2.81B, 9.7% CAGR to 2033), Mordor Intelligence ($2.78B, 8.96% CAGR to 2031), and MarketsAndMarkets ($3.94B, 19.5% CAGR to 2032) — the underlying market definition and growth trajectory are not well triangulated despite similar 2025 base figures.

absences: No source quantifies the actual share (%) of deployed/shipped GPU racks using AC rack-PDU architecture versus DC busbar architecture in the field — only vendor reference-architecture options were found, not real-world adoption shares.

Omdia's Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis 2025, Rack PDU Market Tracker 2025, and Rack PDU Annual Tracker 2024 — the reports most likely to contain vendor-share and AI/GPU-driven attach-rate breakdowns — are paywalled, so no numeric attach-rate or vendor-share figures were retrievable.

No query result quantifies what fraction of the $2.81B–$3.94B PDU market (2025) is specifically attributable to AI/GPU workloads versus general-purpose IT, despite multiple reports naming AI/GPU as a growth driver.

No data found on real-world enterprise on-premise adoption rates of DC busbar power versus traditional rack PDUs, as opposed to hyperscale/AI-factory deployments where the NVIDIA reference architectures are aimed.
